config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas yang jelas agar kepentingan masyarakat tidak berubah menjadi alasan untuk menekan minoritas atau membenarkan penyalahgunaan kuasa. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi seharusnya dicapai lewat keseimbangan, bukan dengan mengorbankan kebebasan dan martabat orang per orang. Dalam pengalaman saya melihat kehidupan sosial, kebijakan yang paling sehat adalah yang melindungi masyarakat sekaligus menghormati hak dasar tiap individu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over the individual by default, because in the United States that logic has too often been used to excuse overreach and crush unpopular minorities. Public safety and the common good matter, but they have to be pursued within firm limits that protect speech, due process, privacy, and bodily autonomy. If society always wins, then individual rights become conditional privileges instead of real rights. The better standard is that society can ask a lot from people, but it must justify any restriction very carefully and narrowly.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu secara umum. Dari pengalaman saya melihat Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, bahkan kadang menekan kelompok kecil atau orang yang tidak punya kuasa. Kepentingan sosial memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar seperti kebebasan, martabat, dan perlindungan dari penyalahgunaan wewenang. Jadi, yang tepat bukan masyarakat selalu menang, melainkan ada keseimbangan yang ketat dan alasan yang benar-benar kuat untuk setiap pembatasan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think the interests of society should take priority over individual rights as a general rule. From a U.S. perspective, once you give society the default trump card, it becomes far too easy for governments, majorities, or institutions to justify censorship, discrimination, and unnecessary intrusion in the name of the common good. That said, individual rights are not absolute in every case, because public health, safety, and fraud prevention can justify narrow limits, but those limits need strong evidence and strict boundaries. So for me the presumption should stay with the individual, and society has to earn any exception.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis berada di atas hak individu. Dalam konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi ketertiban” atau “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, dan yang paling sering terdampak justru kelompok kecil atau orang yang tidak punya perlindungan kuat. Hak individu memang tidak absolut, tetapi pembatasannya harus sangat sempit, jelas, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan, bukan dijadikan dalih untuk menekan perbedaan. Jadi menurut saya, masyarakat penting, tetapi hak dasar individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think the interests of society should take priority over individual rights as a general principle. From a U.S. perspective, that idea has too often been used to justify censorship, forced conformity, and government overreach, especially when the people being sacrificed are unpopular minorities or dissenters. Society does have legitimate needs, like public safety and disease control, but those are exceptions that have to be narrowly tailored and strongly justified, not a default rule. If we let “the common good” automatically outrank individual rights, then rights stop being protections and become permissions granted by whoever has power.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
